evidence: Full-sketch endpoint (handleFullSketchForType) emits per-relation maps with {name, cql, collocate_pos_group, collocations} — no 'id', no 'total_matches'
Single-relation endpoint (handleRelationQueryForPattern) emits {id, name, collocations, total_matches} — no 'cql', no 'collocate_pos_group'
Dep-sketch (buildRelationResponse) emits {id, name, description, deprel, total_matches, collocations} — a third, partially overlapping shape

In explore/explore-multi, 'relation' is echoed inside 'parameters' as the enum type name (e.g. 'SURFACE'); in /api/concordance/examples it is echoed at the top level as the relation ID (e.g. 'noun_adj_predicates')
wrapWithErrorHandling maps IAE→400 and IOException→500 uniformly across all handlers; requireParam/parseIntParam/parseDoubleParam used consistently; 'status: ok' present in every success response

strengths: Uniform error contract: wrapWithErrorHandling centralises IAE→400, IOE→500, unexpected→500 across every registered endpoint
Parameter parsing infrastructure (requireParam, parseIntParam, parseDoubleParam, parseQueryParams) is used consistently in every handler — no ad-hoc parsing
'status: ok' is present in every JSON success response across all handlers
Sibling endpoints /api/semantic-field/examples and /api/concordance/examples mirror each other using the same 'seed'/'collocate'/'top' parameter names
POST-only constraint is applied consistently to both /api/bcql and /api/visual/radial, with matching enforcement via registerPostHandler
api_surface_coherence.issue_character: The inconsistencies are not random noise but structural: the three sketch endpoint variants each return a different per-relation response shape, and the 'relation' echo appears at different nesting levels with different value types across endpoints — a developer must read each implementation to know what fields to expect.
api_surface_coherence.score_rationale: The cross-cutting concerns (error handling, param parsing, status fields) are executed with high consistency, which places the baseline comfortably above 60. However, the per-relation response shape divergence between full-sketch, single-relation, and dep-sketch endpoints is the exact type of 'why is this different?' friction the 80-anchor describes, and the relation-echo inconsistency (enum type name vs relation ID, nested vs top-level) compounds it. A developer building a client against these endpoints would encounter enough irregularities that they cannot predict the exact shape without consulting implementation details, warranting a score in the low 70s rather than the 80-range.

issues.summary: Per-relation response shape differs across full-sketch, single-relation, and dep-sketch endpoints
evidence: handleFullSketchForType (line ~122-127) puts {name, cql, collocate_pos_group, collocations} — missing 'id' and 'total_matches'
handleRelationQueryForPattern (line ~220-230) puts {id, name, collocations, total_matches} — missing 'cql' and 'collocate_pos_group'
buildRelationResponse (line ~268-277) for dep sketch puts {id, name, description, deprel, total_matches, collocations} — yet another shape
issues.suggestion: Define a canonical per-relation response shape (e.g. {id, name, description, cql, collocate_pos_group, total_matches, collocations}) and use it in all three code paths; omit type-specific fields (deprel) via null/absent rather than forking the shape. Extract a static helper buildSurfaceRelationMap(RelationConfig, List, List) so all three callers assemble identical base fields.

issues.summary: 'relation' field in response bodies echoes enum type name in explore endpoints but relation ID in concordance endpoint
evidence: ExplorationHandlers line 223: paramsUsed.put("relation", relationType) where relationType = relationConfig.relationType().orElseThrow().name() — yields e.g. "SURFACE"
ConcordanceHandlers line 77: response.put("relation", req.relation()) where req.relation() is the raw relation ID from the request — yields e.g. "noun_adj_predicates"
Additionally the explore echo is nested under a 'parameters' sub-object while the concordance echo is at the top level
issues.suggestion: Echo the relation ID (e.g. "noun_adj_predicates") consistently in all response bodies. If the relation type (SURFACE/DEP) is also needed, add a separate 'relation_type' field rather than overloading 'relation'. Align nesting: either always embed it under 'parameters' or always at the top level.

root_causes: The three sketch endpoint variants (full, single-relation, dep) were evolved independently rather than sharing a single response-shape builder, causing fields to appear or disappear depending on which code path assembled the map.
The 'relation' field was added to each endpoint independently without a shared convention for whether to echo the relation ID or the relation type enum name.
